channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sony tipped to finally launch first new full-frame camera of 2024 soon – here’s what it could be
item.description: Sony's otherwise quiet year could finish with a bang if rumors of a new full-frame Alpha camera being launched in 2024 turn out to be true.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: An electric aircraft the military has its eyes on can take off with only 150 feet of runway
item.description: Natural disasters like Hurricane Helene in North Carolina illustrate the need for aircraft capable of accessing remote or disaster-stricken areas with minimal infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Racial Covenants in Santa Clara County: Mapping and Erasing Discriminatory Deeds
item.description: Researchers at Stanford University and Princeton University collaborate with the County of Santa Clara to map and redact racial covenants using AI, highlighting persistent housing discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Miss having a dedicated work phone? Here’s how to configure your iPhone to separate business from personal use
item.description: Smartphones have enabled us to attend to work from nearly anywhere. But their productivity power also frequently encroaches on our personal lives—making it too easy to reply to business emails or Slack messages when we should be focused on dinner with the fam.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts judge rules Meta must face state's social media lawsuit
item.description: A Massachusetts Superior Court judge has ruled Meta must face a state lawsuit, alleging the company knowingly designed and deployed features harmful to young online users.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Data center provider fakes Tier 4 data center certificate to bag $11M SEC deal
item.description: Deepak Jain, CEO of a Maryland-based IT services firm, has been indicted for fraud and making false statements after allegedly falsifying a Tier 4 data center certification to secure a $10.7 million contract with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Treasury kept $4B away from fraudsters this year. It plans to triple that by 2029
item.description: Department officials are building on these fraud-prevention efforts, and expect to prevent $12 billion in improper payments annually by 2029. The post Treasury kept $4B away from fraudsters this year. It plans to triple that by 2029 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: One Direction was the internet's first boy band, and Liam Payne its grounding force
item.description: One Direction member Liam Payne was found dead this week after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, Argentina, at just 31.
item.dc:creator: Maria Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Scientists show how sperm and egg come together like a key in a lock
item.description: Scientists have pinpointed how sperm and egg fuse together across the animal kingdom, from fish to people.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step
item.description: Jeff Baur, the product director for logistics IT for the Navy’s PEO-MLB, said ensuring the new platform meets the users’ needs is a top priority. The post Navy logistics modernization effort ready to take next step first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Chinese cybersecurity association urges review of Intel products
item.description: The Cybersecurity Association of China (CSAC) has urged a security review of Intel products sold in the country, claiming the US semiconductor firm poses ongoing threats to China’s national security and interests.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Secure Azure Kubernetes with Advanced Container Networking Services
item.description: Microsoft’s Azure Kubernetes Service is designed to take care of much of the day-to-day management of Kubernetes, simplifying a lot of the complexity that goes with building and running large-scale, cloud-native applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to use Task.WhenEach in .NET 9
item.description: The upcoming release of .NET 9 introduces the Task.WhenEach method, which enables developers to handle asynchronous tasks more elegantly. The Task.WhenEach method allows us to iterate through tasks as they complete, without having to call Task.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How Microsoft became a Big Tech choirboy
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Department of Justice have aggressively targeted Big Tech, suing Meta, Google, Amazon, and Apple for antitrust violations. And it’s not doing so in a small way — it’s filed multiple law suits against Google, for example.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cato Networks extends SASE platform with digital experience monitoring
item.description: Cato Networks today introduced digital experience monitoring (DEM) capabilities that will augment the company’s cloud SASE platform with application traffic and network performance data that IT operations teams need to optimize end-user experiences.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SUSE Edge upgrade targets Kubernetes and Linux at the edge
item.description: Open source and Linux platform vendor SUSE is looking to help organizations solve some of the complexity and challenges of edge computing with the company’s SUSE Edge 3.1 release, announced today.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Open source package entry points could be used for command jacking
item.description: Open source application packages, including those in Python and JavaScript, have a vulnerability in their entry points that could be used by threat actors to execute malicious code to steal data, plant malware, and more.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving digital landscape, businesses face constant pressure to adapt or risk being left behind. The strategic use of IT-enabled information management capability (IMC) is no longer just an advantage — it’s a necessity.
item.dc:creator: Info World
